commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=054a8d60a8b4e5a11dfe757379164a1460825908)
commands.review_summary:
The PR parses Telegram reaction directives into structured channel data, calls Telegram native reactions from reply/outbound paths, and adds focused normalization and Telegram tests.

PR surface: Source +123, Tests +206. Total +329 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main and v2026.6.8 lack reaction directive parsing, and the normal Telegram assistant dispatch path still requires text or media before payload delivery.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor live Telegram proof plus maintainer review after the dispatch-path gap is fixed, so it is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(telegram): honor outbound reaction directives
target.body:
## Summary

- Parse Telegram reaction directives without leaking them into visible chat text.
- Preserve reaction intent in structured `channelData.telegram.reaction`.
- Support reaction-only replies when a valid target exists.
- Use Telegram's native reaction API instead of simulating the behavior in text.
- Reject missing targets and unsuccessful reaction delivery.

## Linked context

Closes #71140

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Telegram reaction directives now normalize into native reaction payloads instead of leaking as visible text or being dropped.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw Telegram SUT on macOS source checkout, with focused Vitest coverage for the production reply normalization and Telegram delivery paths.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/payloads.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       134 passed (134)
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards
```

- Observed result after fix: Reaction directives are stripped from visible text, reaction-only payloads reach the Telegram reaction API, and rejected or targetless reactions are not reported as successful delivery.
- What was not tested: A live Telegram account was not exercised from this source checkout. The linked issue contains live Telegram reproduction and prior installed-build validation.
- Negative control / before evidence: Current `main` does not parse reaction directives into structured channel data and rejects reaction-only replies as missing text/media.

## Tests and validation

- Targeted Vitest: 134 tests passed
- Focused core and Telegram lint passed
- Formatting check passed
- `git diff --check` passed
- Core-to-plugin boundary check passed

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
- Highest-risk area: Telegram reaction-only delivery
- Mitigation: Focused parser, normalization, adapter, invalid-reaction, and delivery regression tests

## Current review state

- AI-assisted: Yes, Codex
- Next action: Awaiting maintainer review
- Bot comments addressed: None yet
target.author: hugenshen
